preamble: De-affrication of [ts] to [s] is a common historical development, where the stop component of the affricate is lost, leaving only the fricative.

examples.note: Latin centum evolved into Old French [tsent], which subsequently de-affricated to [s] in Middle and Modern French.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
